title: Henry Augustus Rowland
body: Henry Augustus Rowland (November 27, 1848 – April 16, 1901) was an American physicist and Johns Hopkins educator.  Between 1899 and 1901 he served as the first president of the American Physical Society.  He is remembered for the high quality of the diffraction gratings he made and for the work he did with them on the solar spectrum.
categories: American fellows of the Royal Society
Foreign members of the Royal Society
Members of the American Philosophical Society
Presidents of the American Physical Society
